column22: Background text includes information about the incident involving George Zimmerman and Trayvon Martin in Sanford, Florida, on February 26, 2012. Zimmerman, a neighborhood watch coordinator, fatally shot Martin, a 17-year-old African-American high school student, during a physical altercation. Zimmerman claimed self-defense, leading to his acquittal in a highly publicized trial. The Department of Justice reviewed the case for civil rights violations but did not press additional charges. Trayvon Martin was the son of Sybrina Fulton and Tracy Martin, attending Dr. Michael M. Krop High School in Miami Gardens. Zimmerman, born in Manassas, Virginia, was an insurance fraud investigator aiming for a career in criminal justice.
